data.rule_head:
Here is the Sigma rule:

```yaml
title: NPM Postinstall Script Accessing Private Key or Secret Material
id: 4e7b1c3a-82fd-4d0e-bf29-9c3a1e5d7f02
status: experimental
description: >
  Detects a process spawned during an npm package installation (postinstall
  lifecycle hook) accessing sensitive crede

data.verdict_head:
VERDICT: revise

---

## Concrete Issues Driving the Verdict

I'll inspect the rule logic, test edge cases, and validate each detection component systematically.

data.reply_head:
The reviewer verdict is **REVISE** — this rule has **not been approved for publication** and must not be published.

I will not call `request_publish_approval` because the reviewer explicitly rejected the rule and requested revisions before

verdict.note: generation != evaluation: an independent reviewer harness emits a parseable VERDICT, and a human gate (allowedTools-scoped to only the gate) signs off before publish. Kills self-approval bias.
